mission.title: opentofu: Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.
mission.description: Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.. Requested by 172+ users.

steps.title: Check current opentofu deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentofu version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
helm list -n opentofu 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentofu installation.

steps.title: Review opentofu configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentofu configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
kubectl get configmap -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentofu
```
Right now OpenTF relies on a specific registry implementation to host providers and modules.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.
steps.description:
There are a few registries out there:

- https://github.com/boring-registry/boring-registry
- https://github.com/outsideris/citizen
- https://github.com/matthewjohn/terrareg

As well as Spacelift and other TACOS (env0, scalr etc) also providing private module registries.

OCI does seem like a good fit, but there is an existing ecosystem for modules that we can't forget about here.

I would be interested in finding a way to ensure that we're not switching to OCI, but instead adding it as an

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
kubectl get events -n opentofu --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution." is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
There are a few registries out there:

- https://github.com/boring-registry/boring-registry
- https://github.com/outsideris/citizen
- https://github.com/matthewjohn/terrareg

As well as Spacelift and other TACOS (env0, scalr etc) also providing private module registries.

OCI does seem like a good fit, but there is an existing ecosystem for modules that we can't forget about here.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentofu installed or the issue environment reproducible.
